title: leucheria lithospermifolia subsp. integrifolia
intro: Leucheria lithospermifolia subsp. integrifolia, also known as the mountain broom or Leucheria, is a shrubby plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It is native to New Zealand, where it is found in the mountainous regions and rocky outcrops. Leucheria lithospermifolia subsp. integrifolia has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, and it is often prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal use.
